atoms.semantic_claim: The old component builder constructs the transaction pool before EVM configuration and calls `build_evm_with_pool`, giving EVM/block-verification construction direct access to the pool so verifier logic can coordinate with transaction-pool state.

destinations.evidence: The merge notes explicitly say not to carry forward the old build_evm_with_pool shim. They direct custom execution to the current ExecutorBuilder shape and say async/scheduled txpool behavior should map to PoolBuilder<Node, Evm>, transaction validation, or pool maintenance because the pool now receives the builder-created EVM config directly.

Current component construction builds evm_config with executor_builder.build_evm(context), then calls pool_builder.build_pool(context, evm_config.clone()), then wires network and payload builder with the pool and EVM config. This is the intended v2 ownership direction: EVM config into pool, not pool into EVM construction.

The current PoolBuilder is generic over Evm and build_pool receives evm_config. Concrete maintenance helpers still require TransactionPoolExt and spawn maintain_transaction_pool_future on the provider canonical_state_stream.

When the pool receives canonical-state changes, it updates the normal txpool, processes canonical async commitments into the async lifecycle pool, processes canonical async settlements/deliveries, and cleans up expired async entries. This proves the old pool-aware async lifecycle work moved to pool maintenance rather than EVM construction.

atoms.equivalence_argument: Confirmed after mechanical review: the old pool-before-EVM build_evm_with_pool path is intentionally not carried forward. MERGE_NOTES directs the port to the v2 shape where EVM config is built first and passed into PoolBuilder<Node, Evm>. The async pool lifecycle that needed pool state is handled in transaction-pool maintenance and payload code, not EVM construction. The separate synchronous blocking-order gap is tracked explicitly in file-0194-atom-0001 and file-0197-atom-0001.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: For the residual ordering concern, test file-0194/file-0197 behavior directly: canonical async commitment processing must complete before consumers that require immediate result submission semantics.
atoms.notes: Reviewed 2026-05-11: no unresolved build_evm_with_pool proof remains. The hook is an intentional old-API omission; the real remaining FSM question is the blocking canonical-state handler, tracked separately.

summary: Reviewed and downgraded: old pool-before-EVM component order and build_evm_with_pool are intentionally absent per MERGE_NOTES. Pool-aware lifecycle work moved to PoolBuilder<Node, Evm>, txpool maintenance, and payload code; synchronous blocking ordering remains separately confirmed in files 194 and 197.
notes: Reviewed 2026-05-11 against current port code and MERGE_NOTES.
MERGE_NOTES explicitly says not to carry forward the old build_evm_with_pool shim.
Current ComponentsBuilder builds evm_config first, then passes evm_config into pool_builder.build_pool.
Transaction-pool async lifecycle work is in pool maintenance/on_canonical_state_change, not EVM construction.
Residual ordering gap is tracked in file-0194-atom-0001 and file-0197-atom-0001, not this old shim row.
